commit.message:
feat: connector test, chat UX, and tool chain fixes (#54)

* feat: global connectors page, secret resolution fix, per-tool test results

- Implement global Data Connectors page listing connectors across all assistants
- Fix {{secret:*}} resolution in connector test and conversation-engine runtime
- Add test parameter input forms based on tool inputSchema
- Per-tool test results with clear button (results persist across tests)
- Add i18n keys for new UI elements (EN/PT-BR)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add markdown rendering to chat messages

- Create reusable MarkdownMessage component using react-markdown + remark-gfm
- Render assistant messages with markdown in all 4 chat locations
- User messages remain plain text (whitespace-pre-wrap)
- Fix VCOM connector timeout from 120s to 20s (API GW 29s hard limit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use Lambda Function URL for processMessage to bypass API GW 29s limit

- Switch processMessage from REST API to Function URL (no timeout limit)
- Add JWT verification directly in handler via aws-jwt-verify
- Add markdown rendering to all chat UIs using react-markdown + remark-gfm
- Add fetch timeouts and better error messages in BFF route
- Reduce VCOM connector timeout from 120s to 10s in DynamoDB
- Keep other conversation-engine endpoints on REST API with Lambda authorizer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: i18n error messages + progressive thinking indicator in chat

- Replace hardcoded English error messages with i18n error codes
- BFF returns error codes (TIMEOUT, INTERNAL_ERROR, etc.), frontend resolves to locale
- Add progressive thinking indicator: Processing → Analyzing → Consulting → Almost done
- Add all new i18n keys in EN and PT-BR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: send follow-up messages while assistant is processing

Follow-up status updates are sent as real assistant messages (not just
a loading indicator), so the same pattern works on WhatsApp where
there is no UI spinner. Messages appear at 5s, 15s, and 30s intervals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert to REST API for processMessage (Function URL blocked)

Function URL returned 403 due to account-level public access restriction.
Reverted processMessage to REST API with timeout=29s to match API GW limit.
VCOM connector timeout was already reduced to 10s — total agentic loop
(3 Bedrock calls + 3 VCOM calls) should fit within 29s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add time budget guard to agentic loop (26s before API GW cutoff)

When the agentic loop exceeds 26s, it now returns a graceful message
instead of letting API Gateway kill the request at 29s with a 504.
Also increased VCOM connector timeout from 10s to 15s so consultar_dividas
(~13s) doesn't get a premature timeout that triggers a Bedrock retry loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(conversation-engine): pass non-2xx tool responses to Bedrock + cleanup

- Add validateStatus to connector-runtime so external API errors (e.g.
  VCOM 400 "debtor not found") are passed to Bedrock for interpretation
  instead of being swallowed as generic failures
- Wrap non-object tool results in { data: ... } to satisfy Bedrock's
  requirement that toolResult.content[].json be a plain object
- Make TIME_BUDGET_MS configurable via env var (default 26s)
- Remove unused auth-context.ts and aws-jwt-verify dep (Function URL
  revert leftover)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix evaluation: 5-fold CV, remove support column, add plain-text output

- Step 6b: replace 80/20 train/test split with 5-fold stratified
  cross-validation on all 286 seed sentences; final model trained
  on full seed set for corpus application. Resolves low record count
  in confusion matrix (~57 → 286).
- Steps 6a and 6b: remove support column from classification report;
  show Precision, Recall, F1 only.
- Steps 6a and 6b: print plain-text confusion matrix after display()
  for easy copy-paste into report.
- Intro cell: add classification metrics/confusion matrix to required
  outputs table; add copy-paste instruction.

fix: deno symlink em /usr/local/bin para yt-dlp resolver formato 301

GITHUB_PATH não propaga para processos filhos (spawnSync yt-dlp).
Symlink em /usr/local/bin/deno garante que yt-dlp encontre o Deno
independente de como o PATH é herdado. Reverte format string para '301'.

Move style.css under ugi_app_pod_mgmt/web_apps/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Voice fluency: fractal indexing + symmetry fix + anti-repetition

Fractal Reformation v4: chain-walk re-composition with 3-level fractal
indexing (operator → 5D force position → POS). Topic words anchor the
chain-walk seed, splitting vocabulary WITHIN operators by force signature.

Key fixes:
- _detect_perfect_symmetry no longer treats HARMONY chains as "nothing"
  when topic context exists. Only VOID/BREATH-only = genuine zero state.
- Chain-walk results tracked in _recently_used (cross-call persistence)
  with soft randomization from top-3 candidates.
- Minimum chain length (4) + HARMONY fixed-point diversity injection
  using comprehension ops or T* sequence fallback.
- Compilation candidates carry op_chain; winner's chain used for display.
- Sentence experience lattice stores compressed divine code per utterance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tests for 7 coverage audit priorities (855 → 933 tests)

Address the top findings from the 2026-03-09 coverage audit:

- crypto-utils: verifySessionToken length-check short-circuit, deriveSessionToken,
  verifyHmacSignature edge cases, redactSecrets patterns (18 tests)
- stepflow-handler: postCallback SSRF protection — private IPs, IPv6, protocol
  validation, allowlist enforcement, HMAC signing (16 tests)
- session-manager: setModel restart path with alive/dead process (5 tests)
- config: PORT fallback, AUTH_TOKEN file loading, GH_ORGS parsing,
  production CORS exit (12 tests)
- session-naming: no-API-key early exit, no-context retry, scheduling,
  name validation, provider selection (14 tests)
- ccApi: getSupportProvider, getWebhookConfig, getWebhookEvents,
  setSupportProvider success/error paths (9 tests)
- useSettings: theme defaults, round-trip localStorage persistence,
  URL token parameter handling (3 tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: delete confirmation modal should fully close, not reopen edit modal

When canceling or dismissing the delete confirmation modal, it was
setting catActionMode to 'edit' which would open the edit modal.
This caused both modals to appear in sequence.

Changed backdrop and cancel button to set catActionMode to 'none',
fully closing all modals as expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: yt-dlp format fallback + filler CBR + timing

- yt-dlp: 301/300/bestvideo+bestaudio/best — evita "format not available"
- filler: CBR 500k fixo no pipe (garante fluxo constante para ffmpegB)
- remove delay 1.5s inicial (encoderStdin pronto no spawn, sem necessidade)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Universal sentiment thresholds: apply to all methods including ML

- Threshold widgets (w_pos_threshold, w_neg_threshold) and shared
  _score_to_label() helper moved to Step 6, visible to all students
- Step 6b now uses predict_proba() to compute net_score = P(Pos) - P(Neg),
  a continuous [-1,+1] value comparable to dict_score; thresholds applied
  consistently across both approaches
- corpus["ml_score"] stores the continuous probability score; Step 6c,
  Step 10, and Step 11 updated to detect ml_score instead of ml_sentiment
- Step 10 charts now show threshold lines on sentiment plots
- Step 11 automated_label uses _score_to_label() for all methods
- Step 12 payload: pos_threshold/neg_threshold sent unconditionally
- Step 13 summary: threshold rows always included (no conditional)
- Sentiment Analysis markdown updated to explain the universal threshold
  design and the net-probability approach for ML methods

No Google Sheets or Apps Script changes required: pos_threshold and
neg_threshold columns already exist and will now always be populated.

commit.message:
Fix multi-target spell targeting (Arc Trail) — wrong card index and missing yellow borders

The frontend never set multiTargetCardIndex for spells from hand (only for
abilities), so confirmMultiTargets sent cardIndex=-1 causing an
IndexOutOfBoundsException on the backend. Also, the targetable CSS class
only checked selectingTarget but multi-target mode uses multiTargeting,
so valid targets never got yellow borders. Fixed addMultiTargetPlayer to
refresh valid targets for the next position like addMultiTarget already did,
and added an early bounds check in SpellCastingService for defense in depth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add self-healing maintainer system with auto-deploy

- Ralph loop: autonomous agent that auto-responds to prompts and processes inbox tasks
- Maintainer session: hidden Claude Code session running against the woodchuck repo
- Deploy pipeline: binary swap with 60s abort countdown, rollback, rate limiting
- Auto-deploy: detects new commits after task completion, builds frontend + backend, re-execs
- Hook injection: teaches project sessions about the inbox via .claude/CLAUDE.md
- WebSocket: raw input mode for xterm passthrough, session_id validation, input length limits
- Frontend: xterm.js terminal, update banner on server restart, maintainer controls in Settings
- Playwright E2E tests for health, sessions, maintainer, projects, and deploy
- Security hardening: input validation, error sanitization, symlink checks

fix: Veo API — use predictLongRunning + correct request/response shape

- Method: predictLongRunning (not generateVideo — 404)
- Request: instances[{prompt}] + parameters.aspectRatio (not prompt.text + generationConfig)
- Poll URL: v1beta/{opName} where opName is already the full model path
- Response: response.generateVideoResponse.generatedSamples[].video.uri

Confirmed working against models/veo-3.0-generate-001.

commit.message:
Add non-ETM MACs to sshd for NIOSSH/ShadowTerm compatibility

NixOS openssh module defaults to ETM-only MACs which causes
keyExchangeNegotiationFailure in NIOSSH-based SSH clients
(e.g. ShadowTerm) that only propose basic hmac-sha2-* MACs.

docs: add TDD remediation 90-day design plan

Three-phase plan to address technical due diligence findings:
- Phase 1 (days 1-30): Security fixes + correctness bugs

Target: 7.5→8.5 moat score, Medium→High execution confidence.
